title: tab.create on the port: create a real tab through the socket, and a real one in the fake
action: Add tab_create to the Herdr trait (src/herdr/mod.rs:34-53), to SocketHerdr, and to FakeHerdr. This is how the phone opens a plain shell in a workspace it chose. PARAMS, narrowed by locked decisions -- send exactly these four and no others: workspace_id (always explicit; upstream's default is 'the desktop's active workspace', which is meaningless for a phone client), cwd (always explicit, per D5 -- never omitted, never left to the desktop's NewTerminalCwdConfig policy), focus: false (per D6 -- creating from a phone must not steal the desktop's focus). Do NOT send label or env; nothing in slices 3-5 asks for them. Upstream shape: TabCreateParams at upstreams/herdr/src/api/schema/tabs.rs:8-19. RESULT: the tab_created envelope carries tab and root_pane (upstreams/herdr/src/api/schema/response.rs:90-93). Return both ids from the port -- slice 4 routes the phone straight into the new terminal and needs the pane id. Extract them by hand off the Value with .get(..).ok_or_else(|| HerdrError::Malformed(..)), the same idiom as snapshot()/ping()/read_pane() at socket.rs:285-333; do not introduce a deserialization layer for one call. THE PARAMS BUILDER IS THE TESTABLE SEAM: SocketHerdr does I/O inline, so there is no way to test the real call path without a server -- exactly the problem cell new-shell-new-agent-1 solved by extracting parse_snapshot. Do the same here: a pure fn that builds the params Value, tested directly. A test that only asserts the trait compiles proves nothing. THE FAKE MUST ACTUALLY CREATE, not return a plausible id: append the Tab, append the Pane (with the requested cwd in BOTH cwd and foreground_cwd, matching the pane() helper at fake.rs:179-187), append a PaneLayout row for the new tab naming the new pane as focused, and insert a screens entry for the new pane. All four, under the existing snapshot lock, following set_status (fake.rs:144-153) as the mutation precedent. WHY EACH: without the Pane the next snapshot cannot see it; without the PaneLayout row the D10 anchor join (wire.rs:216-246) is blind to the tab and the fake's snapshot becomes a shape real herdr never emits; WITHOUT THE SCREENS ENTRY read_pane returns NoSuchPane (fake.rs:205-215) and slice 4's create-then-open flow breaks against the fake while looking correct in review. A stub that returns an id without mutating state would let slice 4's end-to-end test pass while proving nothing -- that is the whole failure mode this cell exists to avoid. UNKNOWN WORKSPACE: the fake returns HerdrError::WorkspaceNotFound carrying the requested workspace_id. Do not add agent.start here -- that is cell new-shell-new-agent-5. TEST NAMING IS PART OF THE CONTRACT: prefix every test you add with tabcreate_ (for example tabcreate_params_carry_workspace_cwd_and_focus_false, tabcreate_fake_appends_tab_pane_layout_and_screen, tabcreate_fake_unknown_workspace_errors, tabcreate_created_pane_is_readable). The verify requires at least 4 passing tabcreate_ tests, so it fails on an untouched tree.

truths: The params builder emits exactly workspace_id, cwd and focus: false -- no label key, no env key
focus is literally false, never omitted and never true
A successful create returns both the new tab id and the new pane id
In the fake, creating a tab makes a new Tab, a new Pane, a matching PaneLayout row and a screens entry all appear, and the next snapshot() sees them
The created pane's cwd and foreground_cwd both carry the requested folder
read_pane on the newly created pane succeeds instead of returning NoSuchPane
Creating in an unknown workspace returns WorkspaceNotFound carrying the requested workspace_id
Every pre-existing test in the repo stays green
artifacts: tab_create on the Herdr trait, on SocketHerdr, and on FakeHerdr
A pure params-builder fn in src/herdr/socket.rs with a test asserting the exact emitted JSON object
Fake tests proving the four appended pieces and the readable screen
A fake test proving the unknown-workspace error
At least 4 tests prefixed tabcreate_, all passing
key_links: CONTEXT.md D5 (explicit cwd) and D6 (tab.create not pane.split, focus false)
src/herdr/wire.rs:216-246 -- the anchor join the new PaneLayout row keeps honest
prohibitions: Do not omit workspace_id or cwd and rely on herdr's defaults
Do not send focus: true, and do not leave focus out
Do not send label or env
Do not use pane.split -- D6 locks tab.create
Do not return a fabricated id from the fake without mutating its snapshot
Do not skip the screens entry for the created pane
Do not skip the PaneLayout row for the created tab
Do not change the workspace's active_tab_id -- focus is false, so the desktop's active tab does not move
Do not add agent.start or any name-retry logic -- next cell
Do not introduce a serde deserialization layer for the response; use the existing hand-extraction idiom

verify: cargo test --quiet && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --lib -- tabcreate_ 2>&1 | grep -qE 'test result: ok\. ([4-9]|[1-9][0-9]+) passed'

trace.outcome: tab_create landed on the Herdr trait, SocketHerdr and FakeHerdr. Socket sends exactly workspace_id, cwd and focus:false and returns the new tab and root pane ids. The fake genuinely creates: Tab, Pane with the requested cwd, PaneLayout row, and a screens entry so the new pane is immediately readable. WorkspaceNotFound now carries the caller's workspace_id on the real path too, not just in the fake. 6 tabcreate_ tests.
trace.verify_output: ORCHESTRATOR'S OWN FRESH RUN, not the worker's report. cargo test --quiet: 186 passed, 0 failed (180 before this cell). fmt --all --check: silent. clippy --all-targets -D warnings: silent. tabcreate_-only: test result: ok. 6 passed; 0 failed; 180 filtered out. Guard grep exit code: 0.
trace.verify_command: cargo test --quiet && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --lib -- tabcreate_ | grep -qE 'test result ok 4 or more passed'
